The M square moved from (34-35, 17-18) to (34-35, 18-19). It moved RIGHT by 1 column when I clicked at (35,19). So clicking to the right of an M square moves it right.
Now I understand the mechanic:
- Clicking adjacent to an M square moves it in that direction by 1 cell (or 2 rows up, need to verify)
- The M square at (36-37, 17-18) moved to (34-35, 17-18) when clicked at (35,18) - that's 2 rows UP
- The M square at (34-35, 17-18) moved to (34-35, 18-19) when clicked at (35,19) - that's 1 column RIGHT
Wait, the first move was 2 rows up but the second was 1 column right. Let me re-examine...
First move: from (36-37, 17-18) to (34-35, 17-18) - that's 2 rows up (36→34, 37→35)
Second move: from (34-35, 17-18) to (34-35, 18-19) - that's 1 column right (17→18, 18→19)
Hmm, the movement amount might depend on the direction or the distance to the click. Let me think about this more carefully.
Actually, looking at the click positions:
- First click: (35,18) - this is inside the row range of the M square (36-37) but above it. The M square moved up by 2 rows.
- Second click: (35,19) - this is inside the row range of the M square (34-35) but to the right. The M square moved right by 1 column.
Wait, maybe the movement is always 1 cell in the direction of the click, but the first click was 2 cells away (row 35 vs row 36) while the second click was 1 cell away (col 19 vs col 18).
Actually, let me reconsider. The M square is 2x2. When I clicked at (35,18), the M square was at (36-37, 17-18). The click was at row 35, which is 1 row above the M square's top row (36). The M square moved to (34-35, 17-18), which is 2 rows up.
When I clicked at (35,19), the M square was at (34-35, 17-18). The click was at col 19, which is 1 column to the right of the M square's right column (18). The M square moved to (34-35, 18-19), which is 1 column right.
So the movement might be: the M square moves toward the click by the distance between the click and the M square's edge. In the first case, the click was 1 row above, and the M square moved 2 rows up. In the second case, the click was 1 column to the right, and the M square moved 1 column right.
Hmm, that doesn't quite add up. Let me just accept that clicking adjacent to an M square moves it in that direction, and the exact amount might vary. For now, let me focus on the goal.
The goal is likely to move all M squares to specific positions. Given the "about 42 target actions" budget, there are many moves needed. Let me think about what the target positions might be.
Looking at the board:
- Blue shape at ~(27,33) - this could be the target area
- Yellow square at ~(4,31) - this could be another target
- Top bar colors: S, M, p, Y
Maybe the M squares need to be moved to form a pattern around the blue shape, or to merge with the blue shape, or to reach the yellow square.
Let me try a different approach. Instead of guessing the target positions, let me systematically move the M squares toward the blue shape and see what happens. The blue shape is at ~(27,33), so I need to move:
- (34-35, 18-19) → up and right
- (36-37, 40-41) → up and left
- (55-56, 46-47) → up and left
- (56-57, 15-16) → up and right
